items.title: Use minimal, trusted base images
items.description: Bloated base images (full OS distros) drastically expand the attack surface with shells, package managers, and unneeded binaries that aid post-exploitation.

items.title: No secrets baked into image layers

items.title: Pin images to immutable digests, not mutable tags
items.description: Using mutable tags like :latest means the running image can silently change, breaking reproducibility and enabling tag-overwrite supply-chain attacks.

sections.title: Container Runtime Security — Linux Capabilities & SecurityContext

items.title: Drop excess Linux capabilities
items.description: Capabilities like CAP_SYS_ADMIN, CAP_NET_RAW, and CAP_SYS_PTRACE grant powerful kernel-level operations that enable escape and lateral movement.

items.title: Apply a seccomp profile

sections.title: Container Runtime Security — Running as Root

items.title: Require runAsNonRoot
items.description: Containers that run as root inside the namespace make capability abuse and escapes far more damaging, often equating to root on the node.

items.title: Build images with a non-root USER

items.description: Broad get/list on Secrets across namespaces lets an attacker harvest credentials and service-account tokens for lateral movement.

sections.title: Kubernetes RBAC & API — API Server & Kubelet Exposure

sections.title: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for ensure the default service account has no granted permissions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for service-account token abuse from a compromised pod
items.description: A mounted token plus permissive RBAC lets an attacker enumerate and act on the API, often reaching secrets, exec, or privilege escalation.
items.how_to_find: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Confirm reachable verbs are minimal and cannot reach secrets, exec, or RBAC objects
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for service-account token abuse from a compromised pod, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Use bound, short-lived projected tokens
items.description: Legacy long-lived secret-based tokens never expire and are highly valuable if leaked; bound projected tokens are audience-scoped and time-limited.
items.how_to_find: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Kubernetes RBAC & API — Service Accounts & Tokens: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for use bound, short-lived projected tokens, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Apply default-deny network policies
items.description: By default all pods can talk to all other pods; without a default-deny baseline, a single compromised pod can reach every service in the cluster.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Test pod-to-pod connectivity that should be blocked
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for apply default-deny network policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Restrict egress to limit exfiltration and C2
items.description: Unrestricted egress lets a compromised pod reach the internet for command-and-control, data exfiltration, and pulling additional tooling.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Test outbound to arbitrary hosts
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for restrict egress to limit exfiltration and c2, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Block pod access to the cloud metadata endpoint
items.description: Reachable cloud metadata (169.254.169.254) from pods enables theft of node IAM credentials and SSRF-to-cloud escalation.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Test reachability from a pod
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for block pod access to the cloud metadata endpoint, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Confirm the CNI actually enforces NetworkPolicy
items.description: Some CNI plugins ignore NetworkPolicy objects, so policies appear configured but provide no real isolation — a dangerous false sense of security.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Network & Secrets — Network Policies: Empirically test that a denied connection is actually dropped, not just declared
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for confirm the cni actually enforces networkpolicy, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enable encryption at rest for Secrets in etcd
items.description: By default Kubernetes Secrets are only base64-encoded in etcd; without encryption-at-rest, anyone reading etcd or its backups recovers all secrets in plaintext.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enable encryption at rest for secrets in etcd, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Avoid injecting secrets as environment variables
items.description: Secrets in environment variables leak via crash dumps, child-process inheritance, /proc, logs, and debug endpoints more readily than mounted files.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Prefer mounted secret volumes with restricted permissions, and confirm env-based exposure is justified
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for avoid injecting secrets as environment variables, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Restrict permissions on mounted secret files
items.description: Secret volumes mounted with overly permissive file modes can be read by unexpected processes or sidecars within the pod.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for restrict permissions on mounted secret files, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Prefer an external secrets manager with rotation
items.description: Native Secrets lack first-class rotation and fine-grained audit; integrating an external manager reduces standing exposure and improves traceability.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Network & Secrets — Secrets Management: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for prefer an external secrets manager with rotation, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enforce TLS on ingress and internal traffic
items.description: Ingress without TLS exposes credentials and tokens to interception, and plaintext east-west traffic enables sniffing after a foothold.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enforce tls on ingress and internal traffic, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify unintentionally exposed Services (LoadBalancer/NodePort)
items.description: LoadBalancer and NodePort services can expose internal applications, dashboards, and databases directly to the internet.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Confirm each exposure is intentional, authenticated, and firewalled; scan node ports externally
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify unintentionally exposed services (loadbalancer/nodeport), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check for exposed Kubernetes Dashboard and admin UIs
items.description: An exposed or weakly protected Kubernetes Dashboard (or app admin panel) has historically led directly to full cluster compromise (e.g.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Probe for default/unauthenticated access
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check for exposed kubernetes dashboard and admin uis, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Review ingress controller annotations for injection risks
items.description: Ingress controllers like ingress-nginx have had vulnerabilities (e.g.
items.how_to_find: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Network & Secrets — Ingress & Service Exposure: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for review ingress controller annotations for injection risks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy

items.title: Enforce Pod Security Admission (restricted) on namespaces
items.description: PodSecurityPolicy is removed; without Pod Security Admission or an equivalent, nothing prevents privileged, host-sharing, or root workloads from being created.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Test that a privileged pod is rejected
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enforce pod security admission (restricted) on namespaces, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Deploy a policy engine (OPA/Gatekeeper or Kyverno)
items.description: A policy engine enforces organization-specific guardrails — trusted registries, no latest tags, required labels, blocked capabilities — beyond what PSA covers.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster audit (Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy): A policy engine enforces organization-specific guardrails — trusted registries, no latest tags, required labels, blocked capabilities — beyond what PSA covers
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for deploy a policy engine (opa/gatekeeper or kyverno), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Harden admission webhook configuration
items.description: Misconfigured admission webhooks (failurePolicy: Ignore, overly broad rules, or a compromised webhook) can be bypassed or abused to weaken or block cluster controls.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Confirm critical webhooks fail closed and the webhook service is protected
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for harden admission webhook configuration, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enforce supply-chain policy at admission (signing/provenance)
items.description: Admission-time verification of image signatures and provenance attestations stops untrusted or tampered images from ever running.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy: Cluster audit (Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — Admission Control & Policy): Admission-time verification of image signatures and provenance attestations stops untrusted or tampered images from ever running
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enforce supply-chain policy at admission (signing/provenance), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security

items.title: Restrict network exposure of etcd
items.description: etcd reachable beyond the control plane allows direct reads/writes of all cluster state and secrets, bypassing the API server and RBAC entirely.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Test reachability
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for restrict network exposure of etcd, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Require mutual TLS for etcd client and peer traffic
items.description: Without client-cert authentication and peer TLS, anyone with network access to etcd can read or modify cluster data without credentials.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Verify unauthenticated/clear-text connections to etcd are refused
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for require mutual tls for etcd client and peer traffic, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Protect and encrypt etcd backups
items.description: etcd snapshots contain every secret and config in the cluster; unencrypted or world-readable backups are a complete cluster breach if obtained.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Verify restore procedures are tested
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for protect and encrypt etcd backups, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Confirm data-at-rest encryption covers etcd contents
items.description: Even with network controls, plaintext secrets in the etcd datastore are exposed to disk theft, snapshot leaks, and host compromise.
items.how_to_find: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — etcd Security: Run `kubectl auth can-i --list`, kube-bench, kube-hunter, and Trivy on images
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for confirm data-at-rest encryption covers etcd contents, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Cluster Hardening & Supply Chain — CIS Benchmark & Control-Plane Hardening

items.title: Run kube-bench against control plane and nodes
items.description: The CIS Kubernetes Benchmark codifies hundreds of hardening checks; running kube-bench surfaces insecure flags and file permissions that manual review misses.

items.title: Harden kubelet configuration
items.description: Kubelet defaults can permit anonymous access, read-only port exposure, and unrotated certs, providing node and pod control to attackers.
